// Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot from typed IR AOT package "native_aot_tests".

pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, Token};
pub type U1 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedBitsInU8<1>;
pub type U2 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedBitsInU8<2>;
pub type U8 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedBitsInU8<8>;
pub type U32 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedBitsInU32<32>;
pub type U42 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedBitsInU64<42>;
pub type U65 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedWideBits<65, 2>;
pub type U73 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedWideBits<73, 2>;
pub type U96 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedWideBits<96, 2>;
pub type U129 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedWideBits<129, 3>;
pub type U130 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedWideBits<130, 3>;
pub type S129 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::SignedWideBits<129, 3>;
pub type S130 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::SignedWideBits<130, 3>;

pub mod native_aot_tests {
    #![allow(dead_code)]

    #![allow(unused_imports)]

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct CompoundInput {
        pub base: super::U42,
        pub limbs: [super::U65; 2],
    }

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct CompoundOutput {
        pub sum: super::U42,
        pub limbs: [super::U65; 2],
    }

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct EmptyOutput {
    }

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct WideRuntimeOutput {
        pub product: super::U129,
        pub signed_product: super::S129,
        pub quotient: super::U129,
        pub left: super::U129,
        pub right: super::U129,
        pub slice: super::U96,
        pub low_slice: super::U32,
        pub updated: super::U129,
        pub unsigned_sum: super::U130,
        pub signed_sum: super::S130,
        pub hot: super::U130,
        pub encoded: super::U8,
        pub decoded: super::U130,
    }

    pub mod aot_add_inputs {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        use super::*;

        // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

        pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError, RunResult};
        use std::sync::LazyLock;
        #[allow(unused_imports)]
        use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
            AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
            ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
            xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
            xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
        };

        unsafe extern "C" {
            #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_add_inputs"]
            fn linked_entrypoint(
                inputs: *const *const u8,
                output: *mut u8,
                scratch: *mut u8,
                context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
            ) -> i32;
        }

        const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 0;
        const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 1;
        static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
            LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![] });

        fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
            std::hint::black_box([
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
            ]);
        }

        /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
        pub struct Runner {
            scratch: Vec<u64>,
            context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
        }

        impl Runner {
            /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
            pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                    )));
                }
                Ok(Self {
                    scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                    context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                })
            }

            fn invoke(
                &mut self,
                inputs: &[*const u8],
                output: *mut u8,
                options: ExecutionOptions,
            ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                    std::ptr::null_mut()
                } else {
                    self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                };
                let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                let status = unsafe {
                    linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                };
                if status == 0 {
                    Ok(())
                } else {
                    Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                }
            }

            fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                    )));
                }
                if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                    )));
                }
                Ok(())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
            pub fn run_into_with_events(&mut self, a: &super::super::U8, b: &super::super::U8, output: &mut super::super::U8, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(a).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(b).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(self.context.result())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run_into(&mut self, a: &super::super::U8, b: &super::super::U8, output: &mut super::super::U8) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(a).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(b).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
            }

            /// Runs and returns output together with observable event records.
            pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, a: &super::super::U8, b: &super::super::U8, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<RunResult<super::super::U8>, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(a).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(b).cast::<u8>()];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::super::U8>::uninit();
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(RunResult {
                    output: unsafe { output.assume_init() },
                    events: self.context.result(),
                })
            }

            /// Runs and returns only output, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run(&mut self, a: &super::super::U8, b: &super::super::U8) -> Result<super::super::U8, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(a).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(b).cast::<u8>()];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::super::U8>::uninit();
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)?;
                Ok(unsafe { output.assume_init() })
            }
        }

        /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
        pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
            Runner::new()
        }

        std::thread_local! {
            static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
        }

        /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                if runner_slot.is_none() {
                    runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                }
                Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
            })
        }

        /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            with_thread_local_runner(f)?
        }

    }
    pub mod aot_add_one {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        use super::*;

        // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

        pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError, RunResult};
        use std::sync::LazyLock;
        #[allow(unused_imports)]
        use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
            AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
            ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
            xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
            xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
        };

        unsafe extern "C" {
            #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_add_one"]
            fn linked_entrypoint(
                inputs: *const *const u8,
                output: *mut u8,
                scratch: *mut u8,
                context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
            ) -> i32;
        }

        const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 0;
        const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 1;
        static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
            LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![] });

        fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
            std::hint::black_box([
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
            ]);
        }

        /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
        pub struct Runner {
            scratch: Vec<u64>,
            context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
        }

        impl Runner {
            /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
            pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                    )));
                }
                Ok(Self {
                    scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                    context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                })
            }

            fn invoke(
                &mut self,
                inputs: &[*const u8],
                output: *mut u8,
                options: ExecutionOptions,
            ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                    std::ptr::null_mut()
                } else {
                    self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                };
                let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                let status = unsafe {
                    linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                };
                if status == 0 {
                    Ok(())
                } else {
                    Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                }
            }

            fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                    )));
                }
                if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                    )));
                }
                Ok(())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
            pub fn run_into_with_events(&mut self, x: &super::super::U42, output: &mut super::super::U42, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(x).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(self.context.result())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run_into(&mut self, x: &super::super::U42, output: &mut super::super::U42) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(x).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
            }

            /// Runs and returns output together with observable event records.
            pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, x: &super::super::U42, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<RunResult<super::super::U42>, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(x).cast::<u8>()];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::super::U42>::uninit();
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(RunResult {
                    output: unsafe { output.assume_init() },
                    events: self.context.result(),
                })
            }

            /// Runs and returns only output, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run(&mut self, x: &super::super::U42) -> Result<super::super::U42, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(x).cast::<u8>()];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::super::U42>::uninit();
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)?;
                Ok(unsafe { output.assume_init() })
            }
        }

        /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
        pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
            Runner::new()
        }

        std::thread_local! {
            static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
        }

        /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                if runner_slot.is_none() {
                    runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                }
                Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
            })
        }

        /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            with_thread_local_runner(f)?
        }

    }
    pub mod aot_assumed_in_bounds {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        use super::*;

        // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

        pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError, RunResult};
        use std::sync::LazyLock;
        #[allow(unused_imports)]
        use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
            AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
            ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
            xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
            xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
        };

        unsafe extern "C" {
            #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_assumed_in_bounds"]
            fn linked_entrypoint(
                inputs: *const *const u8,
                output: *mut u8,
                scratch: *mut u8,
                context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
            ) -> i32;
        }

        const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 2;
        const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 1;
        static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
            LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![EventSiteMetadata { node_text_id: 5, kind: EventKind::Assumption(AssumptionFailureKind::ArrayUpdateOutOfBounds), label: None, message: None, format: None, verbosity: 0, operand_layouts: vec![] }, EventSiteMetadata { node_text_id: 4, kind: EventKind::Assumption(AssumptionFailureKind::ArrayIndexOutOfBounds), label: None, message: None, format: None, verbosity: 0, operand_layouts: vec![] }] });

        fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
            std::hint::black_box([
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
            ]);
        }

        /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
        pub struct Runner {
            scratch: Vec<u64>,
            context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
        }

        impl Runner {
            /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
            pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                    )));
                }
                Ok(Self {
                    scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                    context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                })
            }

            fn invoke(
                &mut self,
                inputs: &[*const u8],
                output: *mut u8,
                options: ExecutionOptions,
            ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                    std::ptr::null_mut()
                } else {
                    self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                };
                let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                let status = unsafe {
                    linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                };
                if status == 0 {
                    Ok(())
                } else {
                    Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                }
            }

            fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                    )));
                }
                if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                    )));
                }
                Ok(())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
            pub fn run_into_with_events(&mut self, a: &[super::super::U8; 2], v: &super::super::U8, i: &super::super::U2, output: &mut super::super::U8, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(a).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(v).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(i).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(self.context.result())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run_into(&mut self, a: &[super::super::U8; 2], v: &super::super::U8, i: &super::super::U2, output: &mut super::super::U8) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(a).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(v).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(i).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
            }

            /// Runs and returns output together with observable event records.
            pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, a: &[super::super::U8; 2], v: &super::super::U8, i: &super::super::U2, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<RunResult<super::super::U8>, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(a).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(v).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(i).cast::<u8>()];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::super::U8>::uninit();
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(RunResult {
                    output: unsafe { output.assume_init() },
                    events: self.context.result(),
                })
            }

            /// Runs and returns only output, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run(&mut self, a: &[super::super::U8; 2], v: &super::super::U8, i: &super::super::U2) -> Result<super::super::U8, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(a).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(v).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(i).cast::<u8>()];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::super::U8>::uninit();
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)?;
                Ok(unsafe { output.assume_init() })
            }
        }

        /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
        pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
            Runner::new()
        }

        std::thread_local! {
            static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
        }

        /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                if runner_slot.is_none() {
                    runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                }
                Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
            })
        }

        /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            with_thread_local_runner(f)?
        }

    }
    pub mod aot_compound_shapes {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        use super::*;

        // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

        pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError, RunResult};
        use std::sync::LazyLock;
        #[allow(unused_imports)]
        use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
            AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
            ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
            xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
            xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
        };

        unsafe extern "C" {
            #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_compound_shapes"]
            fn linked_entrypoint(
                inputs: *const *const u8,
                output: *mut u8,
                scratch: *mut u8,
                context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
            ) -> i32;
        }

        const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 0;
        const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 1;
        static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
            LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![] });

        fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
            std::hint::black_box([
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
            ]);
        }

        /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
        pub struct Runner {
            scratch: Vec<u64>,
            context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
        }

        impl Runner {
            /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
            pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                    )));
                }
                Ok(Self {
                    scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                    context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                })
            }

            fn invoke(
                &mut self,
                inputs: &[*const u8],
                output: *mut u8,
                options: ExecutionOptions,
            ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                    std::ptr::null_mut()
                } else {
                    self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                };
                let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                let status = unsafe {
                    linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                };
                if status == 0 {
                    Ok(())
                } else {
                    Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                }
            }

            fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                    )));
                }
                if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                    )));
                }
                Ok(())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
            pub fn run_into_with_events(&mut self, input: &super::CompoundInput, increment: &super::super::U42, output: &mut super::CompoundOutput, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(input).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(increment).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(self.context.result())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run_into(&mut self, input: &super::CompoundInput, increment: &super::super::U42, output: &mut super::CompoundOutput) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(input).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(increment).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
            }

            /// Runs and returns output together with observable event records.
            pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, input: &super::CompoundInput, increment: &super::super::U42, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<RunResult<super::CompoundOutput>, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(input).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(increment).cast::<u8>()];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::CompoundOutput>::uninit();
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(RunResult {
                    output: unsafe { output.assume_init() },
                    events: self.context.result(),
                })
            }

            /// Runs and returns only output, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run(&mut self, input: &super::CompoundInput, increment: &super::super::U42) -> Result<super::CompoundOutput, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(input).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(increment).cast::<u8>()];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::CompoundOutput>::uninit();
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)?;
                Ok(unsafe { output.assume_init() })
            }
        }

        /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
        pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
            Runner::new()
        }

        std::thread_local! {
            static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
        }

        /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                if runner_slot.is_none() {
                    runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                }
                Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
            })
        }

        /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            with_thread_local_runner(f)?
        }

    }
    pub mod aot_empty_tuple {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        use super::*;

        // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

        pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError, RunResult};
        use std::sync::LazyLock;
        #[allow(unused_imports)]
        use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
            AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
            ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
            xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
            xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
        };

        unsafe extern "C" {
            #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_empty_tuple"]
            fn linked_entrypoint(
                inputs: *const *const u8,
                output: *mut u8,
                scratch: *mut u8,
                context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
            ) -> i32;
        }

        const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 0;
        const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 1;
        static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
            LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![] });

        fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
            std::hint::black_box([
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
            ]);
        }

        /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
        pub struct Runner {
            scratch: Vec<u64>,
            context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
        }

        impl Runner {
            /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
            pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                    )));
                }
                Ok(Self {
                    scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                    context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                })
            }

            fn invoke(
                &mut self,
                inputs: &[*const u8],
                output: *mut u8,
                options: ExecutionOptions,
            ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                    std::ptr::null_mut()
                } else {
                    self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                };
                let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                let status = unsafe {
                    linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                };
                if status == 0 {
                    Ok(())
                } else {
                    Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                }
            }

            fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                    )));
                }
                if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                    )));
                }
                Ok(())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
            pub fn run_into_with_events(&mut self, output: &mut super::EmptyOutput, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [];
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(self.context.result())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run_into(&mut self, output: &mut super::EmptyOutput) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [];
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
            }

            /// Runs and returns output together with observable event records.
            pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<RunResult<super::EmptyOutput>, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::EmptyOutput>::uninit();
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(RunResult {
                    output: unsafe { output.assume_init() },
                    events: self.context.result(),
                })
            }

            /// Runs and returns only output, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run(&mut self) -> Result<super::EmptyOutput, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::EmptyOutput>::uninit();
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)?;
                Ok(unsafe { output.assume_init() })
            }
        }

        /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
        pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
            Runner::new()
        }

        std::thread_local! {
            static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
        }

        /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                if runner_slot.is_none() {
                    runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                }
                Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
            })
        }

        /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            with_thread_local_runner(f)?
        }

    }
    pub mod aot_events {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        use super::*;

        // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

        pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError, RunResult};
        use std::sync::LazyLock;
        #[allow(unused_imports)]
        use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
            AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
            ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
            xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
            xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
        };

        unsafe extern "C" {
            #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_events"]
            fn linked_entrypoint(
                inputs: *const *const u8,
                output: *mut u8,
                scratch: *mut u8,
                context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
            ) -> i32;
        }

        const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 40;
        const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 8;
        static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
            LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![EventSiteMetadata { node_text_id: 7, kind: EventKind::Cover, label: Some("accepted".to_string()), message: None, format: None, verbosity: 0, operand_layouts: vec![] }, EventSiteMetadata { node_text_id: 8, kind: EventKind::Assert, label: Some("A".to_string()), message: Some("bad condition".to_string()), format: None, verbosity: 0, operand_layouts: vec![] }, EventSiteMetadata { node_text_id: 9, kind: EventKind::Trace, label: None, message: None, format: Some("x={} y={:x}".to_string()), verbosity: 0, operand_layouts: vec![TraceValueLayout::Bits { bit_count: 8, byte_count: 1 }, TraceValueLayout::Bits { bit_count: 8, byte_count: 1 }] }, EventSiteMetadata { node_text_id: 6, kind: EventKind::Cover, label: Some("covered".to_string()), message: None, format: None, verbosity: 0, operand_layouts: vec![] }, EventSiteMetadata { node_text_id: 10, kind: EventKind::Trace, label: None, message: None, format: Some("ok={}".to_string()), verbosity: 0, operand_layouts: vec![TraceValueLayout::Bits { bit_count: 1, byte_count: 1 }] }] });

        fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
            std::hint::black_box([
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
            ]);
        }

        /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
        pub struct Runner {
            scratch: Vec<u64>,
            context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
        }

        impl Runner {
            /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
            pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                    )));
                }
                Ok(Self {
                    scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                    context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                })
            }

            fn invoke(
                &mut self,
                inputs: &[*const u8],
                output: *mut u8,
                options: ExecutionOptions,
            ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                    std::ptr::null_mut()
                } else {
                    self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                };
                let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                let status = unsafe {
                    linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                };
                if status == 0 {
                    Ok(())
                } else {
                    Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                }
            }

            fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                    )));
                }
                if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                    )));
                }
                Ok(())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
            pub fn run_into_with_events(&mut self, x: &super::super::U8, y: &super::super::U8, ok: &super::super::U1, emit: &super::super::U1, output: &mut super::super::U8, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(x).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(y).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(ok).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(emit).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(self.context.result())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run_into(&mut self, x: &super::super::U8, y: &super::super::U8, ok: &super::super::U1, emit: &super::super::U1, output: &mut super::super::U8) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(x).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(y).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(ok).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(emit).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
            }

            /// Runs and returns output together with observable event records.
            pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, x: &super::super::U8, y: &super::super::U8, ok: &super::super::U1, emit: &super::super::U1, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<RunResult<super::super::U8>, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(x).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(y).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(ok).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(emit).cast::<u8>()];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::super::U8>::uninit();
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(RunResult {
                    output: unsafe { output.assume_init() },
                    events: self.context.result(),
                })
            }

            /// Runs and returns only output, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run(&mut self, x: &super::super::U8, y: &super::super::U8, ok: &super::super::U1, emit: &super::super::U1) -> Result<super::super::U8, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(x).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(y).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(ok).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(emit).cast::<u8>()];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::super::U8>::uninit();
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)?;
                Ok(unsafe { output.assume_init() })
            }
        }

        /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
        pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
            Runner::new()
        }

        std::thread_local! {
            static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
        }

        /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                if runner_slot.is_none() {
                    runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                }
                Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
            })
        }

        /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            with_thread_local_runner(f)?
        }

    }
    pub mod aot_invokes_and_counted_for {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        use super::*;

        // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

        pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError, RunResult};
        use std::sync::LazyLock;
        #[allow(unused_imports)]
        use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
            AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
            ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
            xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
            xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
        };

        unsafe extern "C" {
            #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_invokes_and_counted_for"]
            fn linked_entrypoint(
                inputs: *const *const u8,
                output: *mut u8,
                scratch: *mut u8,
                context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
            ) -> i32;
        }

        const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 73;
        const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 8;
        static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
            LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![] });

        fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
            std::hint::black_box([
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
            ]);
        }

        /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
        pub struct Runner {
            scratch: Vec<u64>,
            context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
        }

        impl Runner {
            /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
            pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                    )));
                }
                Ok(Self {
                    scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                    context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                })
            }

            fn invoke(
                &mut self,
                inputs: &[*const u8],
                output: *mut u8,
                options: ExecutionOptions,
            ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                    std::ptr::null_mut()
                } else {
                    self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                };
                let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                let status = unsafe {
                    linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                };
                if status == 0 {
                    Ok(())
                } else {
                    Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                }
            }

            fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                    )));
                }
                if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                    )));
                }
                Ok(())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
            pub fn run_into_with_events(&mut self, init: &super::super::U8, increment: &super::super::U8, output: &mut super::super::U8, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(init).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(increment).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(self.context.result())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run_into(&mut self, init: &super::super::U8, increment: &super::super::U8, output: &mut super::super::U8) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(init).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(increment).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
            }

            /// Runs and returns output together with observable event records.
            pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, init: &super::super::U8, increment: &super::super::U8, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<RunResult<super::super::U8>, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(init).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(increment).cast::<u8>()];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::super::U8>::uninit();
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(RunResult {
                    output: unsafe { output.assume_init() },
                    events: self.context.result(),
                })
            }

            /// Runs and returns only output, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run(&mut self, init: &super::super::U8, increment: &super::super::U8) -> Result<super::super::U8, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(init).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(increment).cast::<u8>()];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::super::U8>::uninit();
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)?;
                Ok(unsafe { output.assume_init() })
            }
        }

        /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
        pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
            Runner::new()
        }

        std::thread_local! {
            static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
        }

        /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                if runner_slot.is_none() {
                    runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                }
                Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
            })
        }

        /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            with_thread_local_runner(f)?
        }

    }
    pub mod aot_wide_runtime_ops {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        use super::*;

        // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

        pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError, RunResult};
        use std::sync::LazyLock;
        #[allow(unused_imports)]
        use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
            AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
            ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
            xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
            xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
        };

        unsafe extern "C" {
            #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_wide_runtime_ops"]
            fn linked_entrypoint(
                inputs: *const *const u8,
                output: *mut u8,
                scratch: *mut u8,
                context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
            ) -> i32;
        }

        const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 320;
        const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 8;
        static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
            LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![] });

        fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
            std::hint::black_box([
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
            ]);
        }

        /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
        pub struct Runner {
            scratch: Vec<u64>,
            context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
        }

        impl Runner {
            /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
            pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                    )));
                }
                Ok(Self {
                    scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                    context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                })
            }

            fn invoke(
                &mut self,
                inputs: &[*const u8],
                output: *mut u8,
                options: ExecutionOptions,
            ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                    std::ptr::null_mut()
                } else {
                    self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                };
                let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                let status = unsafe {
                    linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                };
                if status == 0 {
                    Ok(())
                } else {
                    Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                }
            }

            fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                    )));
                }
                if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                    )));
                }
                Ok(())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
            pub fn run_into_with_events(&mut self, x: &super::super::U129, y: &super::super::U129, shift: &super::super::U8, replacement: &super::super::U73, output: &mut super::WideRuntimeOutput, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(x).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(y).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(shift).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(replacement).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(self.context.result())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run_into(&mut self, x: &super::super::U129, y: &super::super::U129, shift: &super::super::U8, replacement: &super::super::U73, output: &mut super::WideRuntimeOutput) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(x).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(y).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(shift).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(replacement).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
            }

            /// Runs and returns output together with observable event records.
            pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, x: &super::super::U129, y: &super::super::U129, shift: &super::super::U8, replacement: &super::super::U73, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<RunResult<super::WideRuntimeOutput>, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(x).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(y).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(shift).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(replacement).cast::<u8>()];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::WideRuntimeOutput>::uninit();
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(RunResult {
                    output: unsafe { output.assume_init() },
                    events: self.context.result(),
                })
            }

            /// Runs and returns only output, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run(&mut self, x: &super::super::U129, y: &super::super::U129, shift: &super::super::U8, replacement: &super::super::U73) -> Result<super::WideRuntimeOutput, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(x).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(y).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(shift).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(replacement).cast::<u8>()];
                let mut output = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<super::WideRuntimeOutput>::uninit();
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    output.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)?;
                Ok(unsafe { output.assume_init() })
            }
        }

        /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
        pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
            Runner::new()
        }

        std::thread_local! {
            static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
        }

        /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                if runner_slot.is_none() {
                    runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                }
                Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
            })
        }

        /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            with_thread_local_runner(f)?
        }

    }
}
